antiReferences.name: Gym-bro protein RTDs
antiReferences.why: They make protein the whole identity and usually rely on performance, physique, and supplement codes. PEP must feel like an exciting drink to order socially, not a workout recovery product.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com
antiReferences.why: Name collision only. That is a separate US energy/probiotic product and must not influence PEP’s positioning, claims, visual world, or copy.

antiReferences.name: Clinical electrolyte and sachet brands
antiReferences.why: They over-index on utility, routine, replenishment, and rational benefit. PEP’s active direction is emotional, taste-led, and social.

antiReferences.name: Meal-replacement shakes
antiReferences.why: They suggest fullness, dieting, and solitary consumption. PEP should feel light, refreshing, and suited to out-of-home drinking.

antiReferences.name: Children’s juice or bargain soft drink codes
antiReferences.why: They would weaken the premium hospitality potential and make PEP feel less adult, less aesthetic, and less order-worthy.

audience: Socially active adults who want a better-feeling drink when they are out.
Taste-led consumers who choose drinks by flavour, mood, occasion, and how the drink looks in hand.
People who are wellness-aware but do not want their social drink to feel like a health chore.
Hospitality customers at bars, restaurants, hotels, rooftops, festivals, dates, and dinners.
Early adopters who like premium ready-to-drink rituals and aesthetically strong beverage brands.
brandFitRules: Approve ideas that make PEP feel order-worthy in bars, restaurants, hotels, festivals, dates, and group settings.
Reject ideas that make PEP feel primarily like gym recovery, macro tracking, or supplement compliance.
Lead with flavour, refreshment, serve ritual, and social excitement; let protein support the story secondarily.
Do not organise competitors or references by alcohol content. Use occasion, ritual, and drink format instead.
Treat strict in-line brands as social-drink peers with premium ordering energy and close ready-to-drink or serve context.
Use positioning-fit/type-mismatch brands for inspiration only when they clarify occasion energy, flavour hierarchy, layout feel, or serve culture.
Move functional performance brands to other-positioning peers unless Lou & Shannon explicitly mark them as in-line.
Move wellness lifestyle brands to other-positioning peers when they are mainly daily habit, gut-health, calm, or self-image driven.
Never imitate competitor logos, label structures, signature assets, or proprietary brand systems.

designPatterns: Experience-led brand world rather than nutrition-first communication.
Premium ritual cues suitable for gatherings and going-out contexts.
Mood and occasion framing that makes function feel social instead of clinical.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong broad fit: social functional beverage with out-of-home ritual potential, close to PEP’s need to make a functional benefit feel desirable in social settings.
inLineBrands.name: Kin Euphorics
inLineBrands.why: Kin is one of the clearest peers for making functional ingredients feel like a social experience. PEP should borrow the principle of mood-led ritual and premium social desirability, without copying its identity.

designPatterns: Cocktail-inspired serve cues that feel adult and hospitality-ready.
Simple product recognition with a strong drink-occasion signal.
Flavour and serve expectation presented before technical product detail.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong occasion fit: an adult social drink ordered in the same moments PEP wants to enter, especially bars, restaurants, and group occasions.
inLineBrands.name: Royal Club Mocktails
inLineBrands.why: Royal Club Mocktails helps define the adult social soft-drink space. It is useful for PEP because it shows how a drink can feel grown-up, flavour-led, and socially appropriate without leaning on gym or wellness routines.

designPatterns: Bar-inspired ready-to-drink cues that communicate premium serve quality quickly.
Occasion-first hierarchy suited to hospitality and events.
A balance of approachability and elevated drinking ritual.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong social RTD fit: a premium ready-to-drink product for the same ordering occasions PEP targets. Different formulation, but relevant broad drink format and ritual.
inLineBrands.name: On The Rocks Cocktails
inLineBrands.why: On The Rocks defines a premium RTD social-drink expectation: easy to understand, occasion-ready, and bar-adjacent. PEP can use this as a benchmark for making a packaged drink feel worthy of hospitality settings.

layoutOnlyReferences.name: Revized
patterns: Use only for website structure if helpful: modular product education, flavour discovery, simple section flow, and conversion clarity.
Do not borrow its brand look, tone, or visual identity. Treat it as layout scaffolding only.

oneLiner: PEP is the refreshing premium protein drink made for social moments — fruity, fun, elevated, and easy to order when you are out.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak for this direction: vitamin drink functionality is closer to routine health support than social ordering.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Functional-protein direction: health and vitamin enhancement are the main logic.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: PEP social should not feel like a daily function system or supplement alternative.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak for this direction: hydration mix format is not a ready social drink ritual.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Functional direction: electrolyte replenishment and dietary performance are the buying logic.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Too rational, clinical, and utility-led for Better Social Drink.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak for this direction: BCAA/energy drink format points to sport and performance rather than hospitality.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Functional direction: energy, focus, and recovery dominate.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Would pull PEP toward fitness and stimulation rather than pleasure and social experience.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Partial product curiosity fit because it combines protein with a social-drink idea, but the brand logic is more athletic novelty than premium hospitality.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Functional direction: protein and active-lifestyle fusion are the main hooks.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Useful to know as category context, but not the desired emotional world for PEP.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak for this direction: functional hydration bottle logic rather than premium social serve.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Functional direction: vitamins, hydration, and health utility lead.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Too everyday-functional and not exciting enough for PEP’s out-of-home social ambition.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate drink-format adjacency as a canned soda, but the mindset is wellness lifestyle more than going-out ritual.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: BLOOM
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle direction: prebiotic health and everyday better choice are the main drivers.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Can inform flavour friendliness, but should not make PEP feel like another gut-health soda.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate social adjacency, but the brand world is more botanical lifestyle aperitif than protein RTD.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: EVER TREE
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle direction: natural sophistication and everyday upgrade are stronger than festival/social energy.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Too subtle and ingredient-led to define PEP’s more fun, fruity, social direction.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate canned soft-drink adjacency, but focused on prebiotic soda and daily wellness.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: OLIPOP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle direction: digestive health and better soda replacement dominate.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: PEP can learn from approachability, but should not become gut-health nostalgia or daily soda.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate canned beverage adjacency, but the primary use case is everyday wellness soda.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Poppi
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle direction: health-conscious, trendy, daily beverage choice.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Too wellness-soda-led for PEP’s hospitality and social ritual focus.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate adjacency as kombucha, but the mindset is wellness refreshment and craft fermentation.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Alive & Well
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle direction: balanced wellness, organic ingredients, and everyday refreshment lead.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Would pull PEP toward wellness café culture rather than premium social ordering.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate canned beverage adjacency, but the use case is calm and relaxation rather than social excitement.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Recess
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle direction: stress relief, modern wellness, and everyday mood management.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Too quiet and decompressive for a fun, festival-adjacent PEP direction.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Strong social occasion adjacency, but currently classified as lifestyle because the emphasis is versatile lifestyle upgrade and at-home/event convenience.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ole cocktails
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle direction in the source table: premium convenience and inclusive lifestyle occasions.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Useful secondary reference for festive RTD energy, but not the core PEP peer set because it is less connected to PEP’s protein-drink distinctiveness.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Strong social adjacency, but more spirits/mocktail lifestyle than ready protein refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ish
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Lifestyle direction: premium mindful drinking and authenticity cues dominate.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Can inform adult serve credibility, but should not make PEP feel too restrained or spirits-category bound.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate adjacency as a premium fermented beverage, but the format and craft cues are more wellness/craft than social protein RTD.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Yaya Kombucha
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Best-fit lifestyle/craft direction: authenticity, fermentation, and natural refreshment lead.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Could soften PEP into a craft wellness drink rather than a premium fun social order.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate adjacency through botanical soda and kombucha, but less direct as a protein social drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Walker Brothers
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Best-fit lifestyle/craft direction: botanical complexity, sustainability, and fermentation culture lead.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Interesting for craft credibility, but not energetic or direct enough to anchor PEP’s social direction.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Strong functional protein format fit, but wrong occasion mindset for this selected direction.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Juuz
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Functional direction: protein content, sugar-free cues, and functional refreshment lead.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Although close on product function, it risks making PEP feel like a protein category product first instead of a social drink first.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: highly relevant social ritual, but spirit-style format is less direct than a ready protein drink.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Lyre's
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: No mindset mismatch; it belongs to the social-drink world. The mismatch is format, not occasion.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Spirit-style product and cocktail-building role versus PEP’s ready protein refreshment role.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Borrow the sense of adult serve culture, premium ritual, and hospitality legitimacy. Useful cues include elevated product staging, occasion confidence, and making the drink feel appropriate for a night out.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: strong premium social context, but more ingredient-like than ready-to-drink.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Seedlip
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: No mindset mismatch; it is a premium social-drink reference. The mismatch is product format and serve mechanics.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Spirit alternative used in mixed serves versus PEP’s standalone protein drink format.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Borrow the disciplined premium feel, mixology credibility, and adult ordering cues. Do not borrow its restraint too heavily; PEP needs more fun and fruit-led energy.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: strong social and fruit-forward drinking occasion, but cider is a different core format from PEP’s protein drink.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Rekorderlig
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: No occasion mismatch; it is relevant to terraces, festivals, and premium fruit-led social drinking.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Cider format versus protein RTD refreshment.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Borrow fruit-led desirability, easy-going social occasion energy, and festival/terrace relevance. Useful for making flavour feel like the hero of the social moment.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: adult social setting fit, but mixer role is less direct than a standalone PEP serve.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Royal Club Ginger Ale
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: No major occasion mismatch; it sits in adult social drinking and hospitality contexts.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Mixer format versus PEP’s standalone premium protein drink.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Borrow adult refreshment cues, bar-back credibility, and the sense that a soft drink can still feel grown-up and order-worthy.

positioningStatement: For socially active, taste-led adults who want a better drink option when they are out, PEP is a premium protein drink that turns light refreshment into a fun, flavour-led social ritual. Unlike gym protein drinks or generic soft drinks, PEP leads with pleasure, occasion, and experience — with protein as the smart feel-good benefit behind the fun.

visual.designConcept: fun, premium packaging, vibrant, festival vibe, fruity
visual.mood: High-energy but polished; playful but adult; fruity, social, expressive, and hospitality-ready. The feel should be celebratory and order-worthy without becoming loud, cheap, or childish.
visual.photography: Show PEP in real social contexts: hands reaching for drinks, table moments, chilled serves, group energy, outdoor events, dates, dinners, music-led settings, and premium hospitality environments. Product shots should emphasise refreshment, flavour, and the pleasure of holding or ordering the drink. Avoid gym, locker-room, supplement-shelf, or solitary desk-drinking scenes.
rejectLooks: Overly clinical layouts that feel like nutrition facts are the main story.
Quiet wellness minimalism that removes the fun social energy.
Cheap party graphics that undermine premium positioning.
Children’s drink cues or overly sweet novelty cues.
Overly formal luxury that feels too serious for the tagline Feel good. Have fun.
Any look that appears copied from a competitor brand system.

voice.tone: Short, social, playful, premium, and confident. Copy should sound like an invitation to enjoy the moment, not an explanation from a supplement brand.
